### Forest: Focus for Productivity {#review-app-1}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      4 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jul 16, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Mukul Gupta_, Jul 17, 2026, 1/5: "Overtime this has only got more buggy and laggy. Not what I expected after buying the premium subscription."
            2. _Antony Filyk_, Jul 19, 2026, 2/5: "They've added subscription-based monetisation after I already purchased premium. Also, there is this annoying red dot notification that doesn't go away no matter what you do. The UI is buggy, for the subscription based service with heavy in-app monetisation this is a really bad business practice. I don't like how this app that supposedly should help battle distractions becomes the distraction itself."
            3. _Jasmin Rother-Struck_, Jul 17, 2026, 3/5: "It is ok. At least you can use it. But there is a bug, where I join a room and I got kicked out immediately. I joint 2 to 3 rooms - always the same. When I do rooms, I got messages from another participants, that they got kicked out of the room. Please fix this. And BTW: Please don't use AI for trees. You are claiming, that you care about the environment yet you use AI for your trees. Atp this app is greenwashing"

   **Pricing and paywall.**

      Review window: Jul 9, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026

### Habit Rabbit: Habit Tracker {#review-app-2}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      1 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 11, 2026 - Jul 5, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _--becca_, Jul 5, 2026, 3/5: "I liked this app for tracking habits and paid to unlock it for the additional habits to track & no ads. it’s simple and easy to understand. but lately it’s been freezing every time I open the app, I think it needs an update to keep up with ios! before, I could restart the app and it would work again, right now the calendar doesn’t work at all and is showing all one emotion and you can’t click anything. update- back to just freezing once or twice per session. but better than when I couldn’t use the app at all."
            2. _Ghufghudstddhytdvgdygvgi_, Jun 11, 2026, 3/5: "Very cute but there is an add after every single interaction to the point where the app is nearly unusable lol"

### Way of Life - Habit Tracker {#review-app-3}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      1 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: May 30, 2026 - May 30, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _quietvest_, May 30, 2026, 5/5: "I have been using this app for years. It is useful, elegant, and flawless. It lets me adapt it for exactly the types of habits I am trying to maintain (or break!) and doesn’t come loaded down with a bunch of unnecessary junk. It has never glitched or had bugs. Would recommend it to anyone trying to form a daily habit or track how well they’re maintaining one. As an additional bonus, it provides graphs of how you’re doing over various periods of time that you can explore when you want. Again, a perfect app."

Three habits for free and no annoying ads or premium reminders. I’ve tried a lot of habit apps and this one is simpler but my favorite. Shout out to having nice color themes for free too!"

### Habit Tracker - Hábitos {#review-app-4}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      5 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jan 27, 2026 - Jul 14, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _QuizoidZ_, Jul 14, 2026, 1/5: "I love the layout, style, and customization. I prefer a “checklist” style which you can set by putting every goal at a 1/1, but it is interesting this kind of goal, things you do once a day, isn’t innate in the system. Also, the app keeps asking me to rate it… almost daily. Once is enough. 🤣 -1 ⭐️ This is a pretty cool app, but there’s some glitch where even after putting in my 5-star review it keeps asking for them. -1 ⭐️🤣🤣 -1 ⭐️ This is like every day 🤣🤣🤣 -1 ⭐️Now I’m down to one star. Maybe they’ll notice and turn off the constant review nagging. 🤣"
            2. _Skullcandy28_, Mar 9, 2026, 1/5: "Can’t delete a habit without the App having a hissy fit and freezing. Setting the goals is just too much brain work. I just want to add when I do one habit on the days on the week but can’t figure it out. It’ll show as 1.43 for surfing once in a week. Why not just fulfil the whole habit on that day. What do I have to put in in the habit settings? It’s causing more hassle than habits 😂 Will request a refund from Apple and delete it"
            3. _J_dster_, Feb 12, 2026, 1/5: "After a recent update last year, App constantly freezes and doesn’t open at all. Had to delete sadly and use another more functioning app. No update has fixed this since"

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      2 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Feb 18, 2026 - Jul 11, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Aybek.A_, Feb 18, 2026, 1/5: "Putting creating habits behind a paywall for a habit creating app is not how you charge for a service"
            2. _A Sprz_, Jul 11, 2026, 3/5: "The app is beautiful. Love the customization. I got a lifetime subscription and am happy to not have to pay annually. Could you please consider adding the ability to increase the font size? It would be perfect then."
            3. _Hermione: Daughter of God_, Jul 1, 2026, 5/5: "So far, so good. I’ve never bright a premium on an app, but this one I just might because it’s not overpriced and seems to have a great layout and system."

### Habitify: Habit Tracker {#review-app-5}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      4 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Mar 23, 2026 - Jun 10, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _ADWired_, Jun 10, 2026, 2/5: "I tried this app for a day during the trial period and liked it enough to purchase a one-year subscription. Unfortunately, shortly after subscribing, the app became unstable. It frequently logs me out, the widgets have never worked (they appear blank), and Apple Health syncing stopped working after the first day. I contacted support and initially received a quick response with some follow-up questions, but after providing the requested information, I never received a solution or any further assistance. I tried reinstalling the app multiple times, but the issues persisted. Out of frustration, I canceled my subscription and requested a refund. My refund request was denied. As a result, I paid for a subscription to an app that does not function properly, received no resolution for the problems I reported, and was then denied a refund. This has been a very disappointing customer experience."
            2. _HectorZeroni03_, Apr 29, 2026, 2/5: "App crashes spontaneously 20APR2025 edit: Communication to the support staff is good as they respond about once a day. That being said resolution of bugs issues is slow (i guess to be expected, but unfortunate due to the 28$ annual fee). The app has so much potential which is why it’s so frustrating with its somewhat blatant lack of attention to detail."

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      2 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Feb 18, 2026 - Jun 10, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _ADWired_, Jun 10, 2026, 2/5: "I tried this app for a day during the trial period and liked it enough to purchase a one-year subscription. Unfortunately, shortly after subscribing, the app became unstable. It frequently logs me out, the widgets have never worked (they appear blank), and Apple Health syncing stopped working after the first day. I contacted support and initially received a quick response with some follow-up questions, but after providing the requested information, I never received a solution or any further assistance. I tried reinstalling the app multiple times, but the issues persisted. Out of frustration, I canceled my subscription and requested a refund. My refund request was denied. As a result, I paid for a subscription to an app that does not function properly, received no resolution for the problems I reported, and was then denied a refund. This has been a very disappointing customer experience."
            2. _…24680_, Feb 18, 2026, 5/5: "I’m not certain that any habit tracker is perfect for most people, still less all. But having tried probably twenty to thirty over the years, this is the one that I’ve at settled with. It’s the only one I’ve found that has a decent UX, can allow collaboration and isn’t *tremendously* expensive (the limitations on the free version of course make that fairly pointless, but the subscription or lifetime options aren’t bad compared with some similar apps’ pricing.) I had a minor issue with some of the settings (due to my misunderstanding) and support were lightning fast with helpful info, so that was a plus."
---

### Habitica {#review-app-6}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      3 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Mar 12, 2026 - May 2, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _fuesec_, May 2, 2026, 1/5: "App is slow to respond e.g. deleting an item from a list"
            2. _exposetruth_, Apr 18, 2026, 1/5: "This bug is constantly coming back where you add to your To Dos and it doesn’t update and it either skips it or doesn’t show it at all. I sent a message to their admin to report the bug and they were rude saying just use the app itself for now. Completely unacceptable and not professional. this app could easily have 4 to 5 stars but it’s no wonder they have 2.4 only. Please have this fixed. I’m not alone so many people are leaving the app bc of this constant back and forth"
            3. _vanskik3_, Mar 14, 2026, 1/5: "A recent update changed the look of the app but resulted in the app constantly crashing. It’s almost unusable. They should roll back the update."

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      2 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Jun 11, 2026 - Jul 16, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _written11_, Jun 11, 2026, 3/5: "It’s a great app and I definitely recommend it to anyone who is motivated by gamification. It’s a little confusing to navigate at first, but great once you get the hang of it. However if you want to collect all the pets and such, you have no choice but to subscribe. Pet quests can be gained if you join a party with people who have subscriptions, but for pets that can’t be obtained through quests you’re basically screwed. Challenges can give you gem rewards, but chances of winning are very slim. I would love if gems were purchasable with coins but were very expensive per gem to encourage people to either grind for them or subscribe. That being said I can see how that could be abused with FCV and spamming habits, so if that were implemented those things would need to be adjusted and capped. But I do think it’s doable and less scammy, and honestly a more motivating option. I’ve fallen off Habitica a lot because when those things are unattainable there’s little incentive to actually use the app TL;DR: Good app overall but in my opinion the motivating factors are not sustainable. Claims not to be a pay to win game but absolutely is one if you want to collect things, which is the main reward system of the game. Needs adjustments"
            2. _AKpopFan420_, Jul 16, 2026, 4/5: "I crashed out from the penalties when having a tough time and ended up uninstalling it for almost 3 years but i’m back because it’s the only task manager that’s actually worked on me. Just wish certain things werent blocked by paywall"

### Atoms - from Atomic Habits {#review-app-7}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      1 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Feb 6, 2026 - Feb 6, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Royal Montgomery_, Feb 6, 2026, 4/5: "i like this app but it has issues and i can't really submit bug reports - the app team wants bug reports to be submitted via the app so they can check, but it would require me to get the gmail app which i do not want. i would change to another email because i do have outlook but...i can't change my email address in the app. for example, today i got a notification that i could save my streak for something i definitely checked off yesterday. not a huge deal but kind of annoying. it is VERY helpful overall but if they could let us change our email or have bug reports that do not require an email app to open it would be five stars."

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      4 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Mar 16, 2026 - Jul 14, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Fjgfnjh_, Jul 14, 2026, 2/5: "I read the book and it helped a lot. I had heard that the author put the book everywhere to help the most people and that was very welcoming to hear. Being suddenly locked out and charged is NOT the principles the book taught, it is greedy scummy practices. I didn’t even make any account it just lied about the free capabilities until I’d been invested for a month. Growing as a person shouldn’t be a subscription. Shameful, I look at the book Atomic habits differently now and that’s such a huge let down."
            2. _Toadanator92_, Jun 2, 2026, 2/5: "I love what the app promises and the design of it but the in application subscription is overpriced and both the free and paid tiers just don’t deliver enough."
            3. _imnotchris_, Jun 15, 2026, 5/5: "I’ve tried a lot of ‘motivation’ apps but honestly most of them had a paywall. This app keeps its simple, but effective at the same time"

### Finch: Self-Care Pet {#review-app-8}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      3 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: May 7, 2026 - Jul 15, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _plantguyhome_, May 28, 2026, 1/5: "I was so excited to use this app, even considered premium, but sadly my data gets lost every time after exiting the app for a while. This means everything is gone and I just get an apology over and over. For an app that’s meant to help people (especially ones with special conditions) this felt really frustrating to me and really killed my energy yesterday, as it has happened multiple times just yesterday. If I hatch a new pet I DON’T get the promised 500 currency + the data gets lost again. 🫤 UPDATE: Now I can’t even hatch a new egg, because the App just turns grey. Whenever I try to access the old data the screen goes white for an infinite time. So I can neither log in nor create a new one. It’s just completely broken on Iphone ig."
            2. _I hate. Finch_, Jul 9, 2026, 2/5: "Finch is bad because it I so slow"
            3. _wolkenflauschig_, May 7, 2026, 4/5: "app is crashing more often lately :("

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      1 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Mr. T-Wrecks_, Jul 18, 2026, 3/5: "I like the app. It has helped me stay focused and productive throughout the day. The only thing that really bothers me is the Shop items. If you’re a Free user, you only get 8 options in Outfit and Furniture to choose from (16 with the free re-roll). But the options are the worst options—they’re typically the most boring/generic options, and they follow the same pattern every time. All the good options require Finch Plus. Why? Free users are earning the same points in the same way as any user on Finch Plus. The items are already behind one “paywall” (in-game currency”). Why put them behind two?"

### (Not Boring) Habits {#review-app-9}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      3 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Mar 11, 2026 - Jun 9, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Kitkattango12_, Apr 17, 2026, 5/5: "I’ve tried a lot of different apps + paper strategies over the years to track habits, and this app has been the best by a mile. It’s easy to set up and stick with, enjoyable to use and visually beautiful, flexible to my needs while keeping me accountable. One underrated excellent feature is that they give you fine-tuned control over the notifications you receive — probably the #1 reason I’ve deleted other habit apps was that they spammed me with a billion distracting notifications, but Not Boring doesn’t create this issue. Price is perfectly reasonable/well worth it. There’s only one minor feature I’d like them to add: I’d love if I could toggle on a “don’t skip 2” option in the settings - I.e. make it so that streaks are only considered “broken” if you miss 2 instances. But otherwise literally perfect."

   **Pricing and paywall.**

      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Bry.864_, May 29, 2026, 5/5: "This app has helped me so much and I think this is the best app simple no adds and with premium it’s amazing. I already have it but this update you just did is a bit laggy I wanted to make a comment about the update.. the change in features are good in 5.6.3 hut after I downloaded it lag instantly when you open the app. There is something wrong with the background but keep it up keep adding support, I have adhd and this does help me a lot thank you."
